conclusion: The addition of calcium to the Tl-Ba-Cu-O system stabilizes the superconducting phase against annealing; annealing at $450^{\circ}C$ in flowing oxygen slightly improves the superconductivity of Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O samples, whereas it depresses or destroys the transition in Tl–Ba–Cu–O samples, and Tl–Ca–Cu–O samples without barium exhibit semiconductor-like behavior down to liquid-nitrogen temperatures.
